steps.title: Identify the key signal in 'by courtesy'
steps.content:
The phrase “by courtesy” signals that the film represents Holiday's life only nominally—in name only—not authentically.
This qualifier reveals the gap between what the film claims to represent and the treatment it actually provides.

steps.title: Match the signal to the answer
steps.content:
Choice C correctly identifies this phrase as characterizing the disconnect between reality and representation that Baldwin critiques. By using “by courtesy,” Baldwin illustrates a larger pattern where filmmakers favored platitudes over genuine engagement.
The other choices introduce elements absent from the text: a later reconsideration (A), critical consensus (B), or earlier films (D).
That matches choice C — C is correct.
